stem: A nurse observes a client who repeatedly injures his forearm immediately before group recreational activity. The client does not seek attention, does not request objects, and stops when allowed to remain indoors. Which function of behaviour is MOST strongly suggested?
A child screams until the mother buys a phone charger, but the same child remains calm when the charger is already available. Which behavioural function is being maintained?

CASE SCENARIO: A 26-year-old nurse reports feeling mentally healthy because she has no psychiatric diagnosis. However, she cannot tolerate frustration, mistrusts everyone, and cannot maintain any role responsibilities. Which statement BEST critiques her definition?
options: Absence of diagnosable illness alone is insufficient because mental health also involves adaptation, resilience, relationships, and productive functioning
Her definition is correct because mental wellness is strictly the lack of mental disorder
She is mentally healthy because lack of psychosis excludes mental illness
Her symptoms prove mental illness only if they are statistically rare

stem: A patient performs a culturally sanctioned mourning ritual involving loud public wailing. The nurse feels embarrassed and labels it abnormal. Which criterion is the nurse misusing?

A 45-year-old man refuses work promotion because he believes advancement will destroy his moral peace. He functions well, is not distressed, and his culture values humility. Which criterion would be WEAKEST for calling this abnormal?
A psychiatric examiner classifies a highly gifted student as abnormal solely because her IQ is far above the mean. Which limitation of statistical infrequency is demonstrated?
options: It ignores whether the rare trait is desirable or undesirable
It applies only to physical illness

stem: CASE SCENARIO: A widow cries daily two weeks after her husband's death but eats, sleeps, attends family meetings, and seeks comfort. Using DIM, why should this not automatically be classified abnormal?
options: Because bereavement cannot ever cause psychiatric disorder
Because everyone deviates from ideal mental health at some point, and context matters
Because sadness is statistically common and therefore always normal
Because DIM applies only to children

stem: A student says: 'DSM diagnosis removes politics from mental health because all disorders are purely objective.' Which response is MOST accurate?
options: Correct; DSM criteria eliminate value judgment
Incorrect; classifications may involve context, history, and value judgments
Incorrect; DSM is used only for personality testing

stem: A man is quiet at work, quiet at church, and quiet at home over many years. Which trait criterion is MOST clearly demonstrated?
A patient is talkative only when intoxicated but normally quiet in all sober situations. Which conclusion is MOST defensible?
options: Talkativeness is clearly his stable personality trait
Talkativeness may be state-related rather than a consistent trait
He has high extraversion by definition

stem: A client interprets every ambiguous picture as betrayal by family members. According to projective-test theory, the responses are interpreted as reflecting:
Which limitation makes objective personality testing vulnerable in a job applicant trying to appear emotionally perfect?
options: Objective tests use only ambiguous stimuli
Objective tests depend on honest and accurate self-report
Objective tests measure only unconscious drives

stem: A nurse wants the test with stronger general reliability and validity compared with projective methods. Which should she choose?
CASE SCENARIO: A 30-year-old patient reports severe distress from thoughts of contamination and spends 5 hours washing daily, losing employment. What distinguishes this from mere obsession?
A person is labelled mentally ill and then becomes ashamed, socially isolated, and avoided by employers. Which effect is MOST directly illustrated?
options: Labelling leading to marginalization and internalized shame

stem: Which example BEST shows medicalization?
options: Calling sadness after bereavement a social ritual
Treating ordinary life difficulties as medical problems under professional authority
Comparing one person's behaviour with their own past behaviour
Using social norms to guide table manners

stem: A patient believes a spiritual vision is real. The belief is widely shared in his religious group and causes no disability. Which interpretation is MOST appropriate?
options: Not evidence of mental disorder solely because it has strong cultural basis
Psychotic because all visions are pathological

stem: In Durkheim's view, why may crime be considered normal at the societal level?
options: Because it appears across societies and time periods
Because it improves individual mental health

stem: A behaviour is common in a society but punishable when performed by an individual. This BEST reflects:
Which example MOST clearly represents intrapersonal abnormality?
options: A shy person remains shy for 20 years
A normally sociable lecturer suddenly becomes mute and withdrawn
A population has varied eating habits

stem: A student says normality is fixed once society defines it. Which correction is BEST?
options: Normality varies by person, time, place, situation, and culture
Normality is identical in all societies
Normality is only a mathematical term

stem: A man scores within 1.96 standard deviations of the mean on a measured trait. Statistically, he falls within approximately what zone?
A nursing student argues that one standard deviation includes nearly all normal people. What is the precise correction?
options: One standard deviation includes about 68.3%, not nearly all
One standard deviation has no use in normality

stem: Which finding BEST supports Emotional and Behavioural Disorder rather than simple social maladjustment?
options: Long-duration difficulty learning unexplained by intellectual, sensory, or health factors

stem: A child has physical symptoms and fears linked to school problems for months, impairing school functioning. This fits which EBD characteristic?
options: Physical symptoms or fears associated with personal or school problems

stem: A young adult has panic attacks, phobias, and social withdrawal without aggression. Which EBD category is MOST consistent?
Which presentation MOST strongly indicates externalizing rather than internalizing difficulty?
options: Quiet sadness and loss of interest
Bullying, cursing, fighting, and acting out

stem: CASE SCENARIO: A 24-year-old who recently left home says he feels lost, lonely, scared, and uncertain about career choice. According to the file, this most closely reflects:
In Erikson's young adulthood stage, the core psychosocial conflict is:
Which statement BEST matches the document's position on empty-nest syndrome?
options: It is always permanent and pathological
It is usually temporary and may later allow enjoyment of new freedoms

stem: A middle-aged woman believes menopause will inevitably cause depression and irrational behaviour. Which response is MOST accurate?
options: Menopause typically causes severe psychiatric illness
Menopause does not typically cause depression, irritability, or irrational behaviour
Menopause is identical to midlife crisis

stem: Which event is MOST consistent with a trigger for a true midlife crisis in the file?
A middle-aged man buys an expensive musical instrument and begins hobbies after his children leave home, with no distress or impairment. The BEST interpretation is:
options: Productive period of mental well-being or re-evaluation

stem: An elderly patient reports sleep disturbance, appetite change, and mood difference but assumes they are physical aging. What concern should guide the nurse?
options: Elders may misattribute psychiatric symptoms to physical problems
Only psychosis matters in elderly care
Mental health services are overused by elders

stem: Which combination BEST explains low elderly use of mental health services?
options: Stigma, ageism, limited primary care training, fragmented services, poor organized support
Excessive psychiatric beds and excessive advocacy
Universal refusal due only to finances

stem: A primary care physician dismisses depression in a 70-year-old as normal aging. Which barrier is most directly involved?
options: Ageism and limited geriatric mental health recognition

stem: Which statement about personality is MOST consistent with the document?
options: Personality is only behaviour, not thoughts or emotions
Personality is dynamic, organized, and influences cognition, emotion, motivation, and behaviour
Personality has no relationship to stress

stem: Gordon Allport's idiographic approach is best represented by:
options: Finding laws applying to many people
Understanding the unique aspects of one individual

stem: A theorist claims human action is mainly controlled by unconscious biological forces. Which philosophical assumption is being emphasized?
A theorist says personality is shaped by both genetics and experience. This position addresses:
Traditional behavioural theorists are MOST associated with which assumption?
options: Humans are passively shaped by environmental forces

stem: Which theory group is generally more optimistic about personality change?
Which Big Five trait is most linked with imagination, insight, broad interests, and adventurous decision-making?
A person dislikes structure, procrastinates, and fails to complete tasks. Which Big Five profile is MOST likely?
A person is assertive, talkative, emotionally expressive, and energized by groups. Which trait is dominant?
A charity worker is trusting, kind, affectionate, cooperative, and empathetic. Which Big Five trait is MOST evident?
A patient overthinks, struggles to relax, has mood swings, and reacts strongly to stress. Which trait is MOST elevated?
Which Big Five trait had the highest heritability estimate in the twin-study figures provided?
According to the document, aging tends to produce which average personality shift?
options: Less openness, extraversion, and neuroticism with more agreeableness and conscientiousness

stem: A 52-year-old shows very little average-level personality change. Which concept does this support?
options: Personality becoming more set with age

stem: Which lifespan pattern best describes sensation seeking?
options: Declines from childhood and peaks at 60
Increases to late adolescence around 18-20 then falls with age

stem: A male manager's ambition decreases over 20 years while autonomy and leadership motivation increase. The document explains this mainly by:
options: Realistic appraisal of limited promotion possibilities

stem: Which mechanism describes therapy-based personality change in the document?
options: Identify inappropriate behaviour, self-monitor, internalize desired behaviour, generalize the trait
Erase temperament permanently in one session
Use only medication to change personality

stem: Freud's life instincts are primarily concerned with:
Freud proposed the death instinct after observing that traumatic experiences may be:
When Thanatos energy is directed outward, Freud described it as:
A person pursues higher education to improve attractiveness, income, and advantage over rivals. In the Freud example, this is explained by:
Which statement about behaviour and the nervous system is MOST accurate?
options: More complex nervous systems generally allow greater learning and adjustment
The endocrine system alone controls all behaviour
Muscles act normally without nervous impulses
Behaviour has no relationship to biology

stem: The correct sequence of response to external stimuli is:
Which statement BEST reflects the document's view of behaviour?
options: Behaviour is always a simple fixed response to one stimulus
Behaviour should be understood through the psychological whole

stem: A patient exaggerates achievements, justifies failures, and daydreams excessively. Which set of futile adjustments is represented?
Which is listed as an adjustment that thwarts development?
A nurse states that character and personality are identical. Which correction is BEST?
options: Character is narrower and refers to ethical quality of behaviour

stem: Which is a characteristic of good mental health?
options: Ability to face disappointments without losing control

stem: CASE SCENARIO: A 17-year-old has persistent low self-worth, expresses need to harm self, cannot maintain peer relationships, and has pervasive unhappiness. Which conclusion is strongest?
options: Marked EBD characteristics affecting functioning are present

stem: Which statement BEST separates subculture normality from mainstream social norms?
options: Subcultures may replace society norms with their own norms
Social norms are always statistically accurate

stem: Most students privately dislike binge drinking but falsely believe everyone approves of it. This is:
A psychiatric label causes others to feel uncomfortable and distance themselves from the patient. Which consequence is MOST directly described?
Which pairing is correct?
The TAT differs from the Rorschach primarily because the TAT uses:
Behaviour provides _______________ from the organism to the _______________.
In biology, human behaviour is believed to be controlled primarily by the _______________ system and the _______________ system.
The four common functions of behaviour are social attention, tangibles or activities, _______________, and _______________.
Mental health refers to cognitive and emotional wellbeing and is about how we _______________, _______________, and behave.
The WHO definition of health includes complete physical, mental and _______________ well-being, not merely the absence of _______________.
The theory of psychological equilibrium views health as a _______________ in the process by which humans interact with the _______________.
A mental illness is also called a mental disorder or _______________ disorder and causes distress or _______________.
The process of defining difficulties as medical conditions under professional authority is called _______________ or _______________.
Deviation from social norms must consider culture, _______________, age, _______________, and historical context.
DIM means deviation from _______________ mental _______________.
In statistics, about _______________ percent lies within one standard deviation, while about _______________ percent lies within 1.96 standard deviations.
Normality for one person is called _______________ normality, while comparison across people is _______________ normality.
The five EBD models are biophysical, psychodynamic, cognitive, _______________, and _______________.
Externalizing disorders involve under-controlled, acting-out behaviours such as fighting, bullying, _______________, and other forms of _______________.
The quarter-life crisis is usually from the late teens to the early _______________ and is associated with becoming an _______________.
Erikson associated young adulthood with the crisis of _______________ versus _______________.
Menopause occurs when menstruation ceases and the ovaries stop producing _______________; the most common symptom mentioned is _______________.
Elders account for only _______________ percent of inpatient psychiatric services and _______________ percent of community mental health services.
It is estimated that _______________ to _______________ percent of elders need mental health care.
Personality originates from the Latin word _______________, meaning _______________.
Allport described _______________ psychology as seeking general laws and _______________ psychology as studying the unique individual.
The two major types of personality tests are _______________ and _______________.
Projective tests assume personality is primarily _______________ and use a/an _______________ stimulus.
The Big Five acronym OCEAN stands for Openness, Conscientiousness, Extraversion, _______________, and _______________.
The Big Five theory emerged in _______________ and was developed by _______________.
The highest Big Five heritability estimate listed is for _______________ at _______________ percent.
Freud's two basic drives are also called _______________ and _______________.
The energy created by the life instincts is called _______________, while outwardly directed death-instinct energy appears as _______________.
Response to external stimuli involves four steps: reception, _______________, preparation, and _______________.
